Football.title: Liverpool star should be Player of the Year contender – and tipped to replace Virgil van Dijk as captain
Football.content:
Liverpool star should be Player of the Year contender – and tipped to replace Virgil van Dijk as captain

Dominik Szoboszlai is having an outstanding season - he must be in the conversation for the PFA Player of the Year award.

That's the view of ex-Liverpool winger Jermaine Pennant, who has insisted the Reds star deserves recognition for his heroic displays this term.

8 Dominik Szoboszlai kickstarted the comeback in Liverpool's 4-0 thrashing of Galatasaray

Szoboszlai has been a shining light in a challenging campaign for his team, who have struggled to replicate last year's title-winning form.

While Arne Slot's side find themselves locked in a battle for Champions League qualification, they produced a stunning performance in the competition's round of 16 in midweek.

The Reds came from 1-0 down in the tie to thrash Galatasaray 4-0 at Anfield, and set up a quarter-final clash with Paris Saint-Germain.

Szoboszlai was once again the driving force, who opened the scoring with a fine finish and was later named Player of the Match.

This saw him take his tally to 12 goals in all competitions this season, with only Hugo Ekitike ahead of him for the Reds, despite the Hungarian featuring in midfield and out of position at right-back across his 42 appearances.

'Dragging us along'

Szoboszlai earned huge praise from ex-Liverpool man Pennant for his consistent, high-quality showings this term.

So much so, that he likened the 25-year-old's all-action displays in the No.8 jersey to club icon Steven Gerrard, who became famed for single-handedly carrying the Reds for seasons on end.

Speaking on talkSPORT's Inside Liverpool, Pennant gushed: “What he's been doing, do you know what? He's wearing that number eight jersey and he's making everyone proud.

“That's like the number seven of Dalglish - the number eight for Steven Gerrard.

"And it was big boots to fill, but the way he's conducting himself and the season that we're having - he is dragging us along.

8 Szoboszlai has been told he is honouring Liverpool's iconic No.8 jersey Credit: Getty

8 The shirt was famously worn by legendary former Reds skipper, Steven Gerrard Credit: Getty

“He is chipping in with important goals, he's covering every blade of grass and I think, at 25, he still hasn't reached his twilight years yet, his prime. He probably will get better.

“So for me, what I'm seeing, I love it. I think he's a great guy, great attitude, and he loves the club and works hard every time he plays.”

Player of the year shouts

While Szoboszlai has earned plaudits for being Liverpool's driving force in a difficult season, his performances haven't quite translated when it comes to individual recognition.

With voting for the PFA Player of the Year award fast approaching, the Hungary star finds himself with less than favourable odds to clinch the individual prize.

Among the favourites for the award are Arsenal quartet Declan Rice, Martin Zubimendi, Gabriel Magalhaes and William Saliba.

Also boasting better odds are Manchester City goal machine Erling Haaland, as well as his teammate, January arrival Antoine Semenyo, and talismanic Manchester United star Bruno Fernandes

8 There have been widespread calls for Bruno Fernandes to be named the Premier League Player of the Season Credit: Getty

8 Jeremy Doku has scored three goals in all competitions for Manchester City this term, but boasts 12 assists altogether Credit: Getty

However, the Inside Liverpool panel were surprised to see Manchester City duo Bernardo Silva and Jeremy Doku ahead of Szoboszlai in the bookmakers' odds.

After being informed of the contenders by host and Liverpool megafan, Sam Ellard, Pennant tipped Fernandes to clinch top prize, but admitted he was stunned not to see Szoboszlai higher up.

Reacting, he said: “The fact that you haven't mentioned Dominik Szoboszlai, I'm a little bit shocked.

“I think what he's doing week in, week out, what he's doing for Liverpool, the goals he's scoring, he should be [a contender], and this is not being biased in any way.

Pennant added: “He should be named in the conversation with those players. I'm not saying he should win it or he's better than what Bruno's [Fernandes] doing.

“But the players that you name, I think Dominik should all also be named as well as a contender.”

8 Bernardo Silva came under scrutiny for having more favourable Player of the Year odds than Szoboszlai Credit: Getty

8 On paper, Szoboszlai's Premier League stats appear more impressive

'Bernardo Silva over Szoboszlai?'

Naming the players he feels Szoboszlai should be ranked higher than, Pennant later said: “The fact that Doku is in there and Szoboszlai is not is a bit strange.

“I think what people are basing this on is if the team's doing well - this is an individual accolade.

“So the team could be playing the worst football, but if you’ve got one player who every single week is putting in a top performance, then that is what it should be judged on, not how good the team is.

“The players that you mentioned, the teams are up there doing well, fighting it out like Man City, Arsenal. But Doku, if you look over the season so far, I wouldn't say he's been a better player than Dominik Szoboszlai.

“Semenyo’s having a great great season, and he deserves to be in there, but again, Bernardo Silva over Dominik Szoboszlai?”

Captain material

Szoboszlai's stunning individual performance against Galatasaray earned him praise from Liverpool great Gerrard, who tipped the Reds star to follow in his own footsteps and become club captain.

8 Gerrard believes Szoboszlai could become Liverpool captain when Virgil van Dijk's time at Anfield is up Credit: Getty

Inside Liverpool on talkSPORT talkSPORT brings you Liverpool like no other. Stay ahead of every tactical shift, manager sacking and transfer rumour by making talkSPORT's Inside Liverpool show your weekly go to for all things Reds related. Subscribe now to ensure you never miss a beat of the action one episode at a time, sponsored by BetMGM. Download the app or visit BetMGM.co.uk. 18+gambleaware.org

Working as a pundit for TNT Sports, the legendary midfielder said: "I like his mentality. He's evolved, and he's grown a lot since he's become a Liverpool player.

"We obviously signed a big talent. He's in the prime years of his career. I think Liverpool are going to see the best of him.

"I hope he stays around for a long time because I think there's a potential captain in there."

And Gerrard's thoughts were well and truly backed by the Inside Liverpool panel, with Ellard arguing that Szoboszlai is carrying his team in a similar way to their old No.8.

Ellard said: “In terms of being an all-action midfielder at times carrying this midfield, Steven's up there.

“But what Dom is producing this season is freakish, and I think Stevie said it himself that [for the] next Liverpool captain, he's there isn't he?”

Football.title: England unveil World Cup 2026 kits as Nike make huge 18-year change to away strip
Football.content:
England unveil World Cup 2026 kits as Nike make huge 18-year change to away strip

England have released their new home and away kits for 2026 ahead of this summer's World Cup.

Boss Thomas Tuchel is looking to end the Three Lions' 60-year trophy drought following back-to-back defeats in European Championship finals.

4 England will be hoping to end their 60-year trophy drought in their new kit Credit: England Football

Just hours after Tuchel named his 35-man squad for the upcoming friendlies against Uruguay and Japan later this month, England have now unveiled their new strips.



England's association with Nike

It will be the fourth time England have worn Nike kits at a World Cup having first done so in 2014, with their partnership beginning in 2013.

For England's home shirt, which is a modern version of the classic white top, a Three Lions design is embedded into the fabric of the jersey.

And in a nod to the national anthem, the words 'Happy and Glorious' are printed on the inside of the collar.

A gold star will be above the badge for England's home kit for the first time in 13 years.



The star, which signified England's lone World Cup win in 1966, had been white or silver on previous Nike kits.

White shorts and socks will accompany the top, with there being no purple element of the St George’s flag - a controversial feature on the Euro 2024 shirt.



In a bold new-look away kit, England will wear red, accompanied with black shorts and red socks.

Unlike with the home strip, a centralised badge is on show.

England have not had a centred badge on the away shirt since 2008, when they were sponsored by Umbro.



4 England have a centralised badge on their away kit for the first time since 2008 Credit: England Football

4 The Three Lions are considered as one of the favourites to win the World Cup Credit: England Football

There is also a subtle pattern in the fabric featuring a blend of lions and stars from the crest.

The kit is rounded off with navy sleeve and neck cuffs as well as a navy stripe on the side.

As it stands, England will wear Nike for one more World Cup after this year's tournament having signed a contract until 2030, with the deal estimated to be worth a whopping £400million.



And a pre-match shirt has also been leaked, which features a stained glass window graphic design.

It also includes a picture of what appears to be a saint on the front of the kit.

Tuchel's side begin Group L campaign against Croatia on June 17.

4 England's pre-match shirt for the 2026 World Cup has been leaked Credit: Footy Headlines

England then face Ghana on June 23 before they round out the group stage with a clash against Panama.

Of course, there's a chance England's new away kit never gets worn at this year's World Cup if the 2022 edition was anything to go by.



The Three Lions only sported their white home shirt, despite having both kits available in Qatar.

There is a chance of a kit clash in all of England's group stage fixtures at this year's World Cup.

Croatia's home kit has traditionally been red and white, Ghana's home kit is white while Panama's national colours are red, white and blue although they are yet to unveil their World Cup kits.

England are listed as the away team in only one of their group fixtures, which is against Panama.

Football.title: Manchester United to more than quadruple Kobbie Mainoo’s salary with teammate closing in on new deal
Football.content:
Manchester United to more than quadruple Kobbie Mainoo’s salary with teammate closing in on new deal

Manchester United are set to more than quadruple Kobbie Mainoo's wages with a new long-term contract at Old Trafford, talkSPORT understands.

Harry Maguire is also in talks over a new one-year deal, plus the option for an extra 12 months as the club look to prepare for next season.

4 Mainoo and Maguire are set to pen new deals at United in a double boost Credit: Getty

The wage bump will see Mainoo take home £120,000-per-week as a base wage with the contract set to be until 2031.



talkSPORT understands that the midfielder is currently earning £25,000-a-week at the Theatre of Dreams.

Sources suggest that Mainoo could pocket more money in add-ons, and as long as his progression continues, United have plans to offer him a new deal in 2028.

Maguire's contract is set to expire at the end of the campaign, but the experienced centre-back has performed well for Michael Carrick.

Both players have also earned England recalls to Thomas Tuchel's expanded 35-man squad.



Maguire hasn't played for the Three Lions since September 2024, and Mainoo has been out of the scene since the exact same match against the Republic of Ireland.

But both are now back ahead of the World Cup as they look to showcase why they should be on the plane to North America in the friendlies against Uruguay and Japan.



Rewarded

Since Carrick's arrival as United interim manager, Mainoo has started in all nine matches, playing the full 90 minutes in all of them, bar the one defeat under Carrick to Newcastle United.

Before that, he hadn't started a game in the Premier League under Ruben Amorim.



Even Maguire before his injury wasn't heavily involved in the team either.

But he has again started every single match under Carrick.

Speaking about Mainoo recently, Carrick was full of praise, but also declared he believes there is more to come from the academy graduate.

4 Maguire has been solid for United since winning his place back in the team Credit: Getty

4 Mainoo has chipped in with two assists from 21 Premier League outings this term Credit: Getty

He said: “He can always improve on a lot of things, that’s the age he's at just for starters.



"There's so much more to come and develop and that's just natural, that's not a negative on Kobbie, that's just the stage he's in in his career, so hopefully he has got a lot layers to improve and keep developing on that.

"I think he's done really well. Not having a lot of football for a period of time to come in and play a run of games and find your rhythm.

"I think he did that really quickly and there's a natural kind of flow really and I thought he was really good again on the weekend.

"Putting in a performance where he did a little bit of everything and controlled the game and spells when he had to defend and it was really impressive."

Equally, the former United midfielder has enjoyed Maguire's performances.



4 Mainoo has returned to the team under Michael Carrick and has impressed Credit: AFP

On the 33-year-old, he said: “Harry’s done fantastic for us, I think it’s pretty obvious to say that.

"He’s had a big impact on what we’ve done, so, we’ll have to see.

“I enjoy working with him, I think he’s been fantastic for this club and we’ll see what comes of that.”

Football.title: Championship club sign 999-year lease to secure stadium future
Football.content:
In a world of clubs announcing new stadiums, we now know that Millwall and The Den will be there to welcome in the next millennium.

The Lions have officially signed a massive 999-year lease at their south London home until the year 3035.

3 The New Den will be leased to Millwall for the next 999 years Credit: Getty

The deal, made between the club and Lewisham Council, will ensure The Den is protected as the historic home of Millwall but also allows them to create ambitious projects at the ground.



With the legal side done, Millwall can expand their facilities which also aligns with Lewisham's vision as a borough.

Additionally, it gives the club the ability to expand their charity, the 'Millwall Community Trust', to have a greater reach in the local community.

The south east London club's chairman James Berylson said: "This is a defining moment for Millwall Football Club and Millwall Community Trust.

"Securing a 999-year lease at The Den gives us the stability and certainty every club needs to build for the long term.



"So much hard work, time and dedication has gone into reaching this moment after announcing the agreement in principle back in May 2024. I would like to thank Mayor Brenda Dacres OBE, and all at Lewisham Council, for their positive and collaborative approach.

"Together, we have secured an outcome that safeguards our home while supporting the wider interests of the borough.



"I would also like to thank the staff of Millwall Football Club and Millwall Community Trust, as well as many supporters and advisers, who have put their heart and soul into this.

"Their effort and commitment has been so important throughout this process, and I appreciate every single one of them.



"The Den and Millwall Community Trust is at the heart of who we are. This agreement ensures that future generations of supporters and wider community will continue to call it home.

3 Millwall are currently sat in the play-off positions in the Championship

"It allows us to move forward with meaningful regeneration and long-term investment – creating jobs, enhancing facilities and delivering tangible benefits for local residents.

"Collectively our ambition is clear: to build a strong, sustainable future for Millwall, on and off the pitch. But above all, to dream boldly, to turn vision into reality, and to unlock the full potential of this great football club.

"My family shares that belief with our incredible supporters. This agreement gives us the platform to make it happen."



Importance of The Den

The new deal will ensure massive improvements for the community including the build of new affordable homes, more jobs and improved public spaces that local people can benefit from year-round.

It means more programmes for young people and families in the area can be made by the trust.

This would be alongside the current programmes they already offer locally - weekly football sessions to kids, mentoring for young people and football teams to help those who are suffering with mental health or have a disability.

3 Millwall could be back in the top flight next season for the first time since 1990

The Lions currently sit fourth in the Championship - which would see them enter the play-offs for a shot at Premier League promotion.



If they do go up, the new stadium lease would allow the club to expand with the size of the league they are in.

The New Den was opened in 1993 after they decided the Old Den was not viable to make safer after the Taylor Report was released following the Hillsborough disaster.

They have fallen high and low in the New Den, experiencing both the Championship and League One football.

If the Championship side manage to get promoted they could be playing two of their biggest rivals in the top flight next season, West Ham United and Leeds United.

Football.title: Arsenal’s Cristhian Mosquera called up by Spain – but could still play at World Cup for another country
Football.content:
Arsenal’s Cristhian Mosquera called up by Spain – but could still play at World Cup for another country

Arsenal defender Cristhian Mosquera has received his first call-up to the senior Spain squad.

Mosquera, who has 14 caps for Spain Under-21s, has impressed since joining Arsenal from Valencia in a £13million deal last summer.

3 Cristhian Mosquera has earned his first call-up to the senior Spain squad Credit: Getty

The 21-year-old has featured 21 times under Mikel Arteta, but has largely provided cover for first-choice centre-backs pairing William Saliba and Gabriel Magalhaes.



He was rewarded for his efforts on Friday when Spain head coach Luis de la Fuente named the defender in his 27-man squad for the upcoming double header against Serbia and Egypt.

There had been questions over whether Mosquera would represent Spain at senior level or switch his allegiance to Colombia – the birthplace of his parents.

Reporters claimed the former Valencia star, who holds a Colombian passport, could switch nations in order to earn a spot at this summer’s World Cup.

But now he has the opportunity to go to the showpiece tournament with the reigning European champions.



"Mosquera is committed, the Under-21 captain, who wants to play for Spain," De la Fuente said during his squad announcement on Friday. "He is having a good season with Arsenal."

In an interview with The Athletic in October, Mosquera said: “When I was 14 or 15, Spain contacted me. “At that time, I don’t remember having any conversations with Colombia.



"For now, I’ve continued my normal process with Spain. But I’m not closing any doors on anyone. I try to carry on with my day-to-day life at my club and let the doors open when they need to open.”

He could be in line to make his debut later this month alongside Arsenal teammates, David Raya and Martin Zubimendi.



A trio of debutants

Mosquera joins three other new faces in the Spain squad in Barcelona goalkeeper Joan Garcia, Real Sociedad forward Ander Barrenetxea and Osasuna winger Victor Munoz.

3 Luis De La Fuente has been impressed by Mosquera's start to life in north London Credit: Getty

Barcelona gem Lamine Yamal will be hoping to continue his excellent form off the back of his display in the 7-2 win over Newcastle in midweek.

The 18-year-old has scored 21 goals and registered 16 assists this season for the Catalan giants.

Dean Huijsen is the only representative from Real Madrid.

Los Blancos full-back Dani Carvajal is working his way back to full fitness after recovering from an ACL injury, however his game time under boss Alvaro Arbeloa has been minimal.



"I don't have to say anything to Arbeloa," De la Fuente said.

"I have complete respect for him. I hope Dani will regain his Euro 2024 form. We miss him, and he's very important. He has to keep working. He knows that, just like Álvaro Morata.

3 De la Fuente is delighted to welcome back Rodri and described him as a 'key player' Credit: Getty

"They need to play, get back to their usual level, and then they'll be with us."

Meanwhile, Tottenham’s Pedro Porro, Chelsea left-back Marc Cucurella, Manchester City midfielder Rodri and Crystal Palace winger Yeremy Pino are the other Premier League stars included in the squad.

Spain squad for March internationals

Goalkeepers: Unai Simon (Athletic Club), David Raya (Arsenal), Alex Remiro (Real Sociedad), Joan Garcia (Barcelona).



Defenders: Marcos Llorente (Atletico Madrid), Pedro Porro (Tottenham Hotspur), Aymeric Laporte (Athletic Club), Pau Cubarsi (Barcelona), Dean Huijsen (Real Madrid), Cristhian Mosquera (Arsenal), Marc Cucurella (Chelsea), Alejandro Grimaldo (Bayer Leverkusen).

Midfielders: Rodri (Manchester City), Martin Zubimendi (Arsenal), Pedri (Barcelona), Pablo Fornals (Real Betis), Carlos Soler (Real Sociedad), Dani Olmo (Barcelona), Fermin Lopez (Barcelona).

Forwards: Yeremy Pino (Crystal Palace), Alex Baena (Atletico Madrid), Ander Barrenetxea (Real Sociedad), Victor Munoz (Osasuna), Mikel Oyarzabal (Real Sociedad), Ferran Torres (Barcelona), Borja Iglesias (Celta), Lamine Yamal (Barcelona).

Football.title: Liam Delap emerges as summer target for Premier League rival with Chelsea open to offers
Football.content:
Liam Delap emerges as summer target for Premier League rival with Chelsea open to offers

Liam Delap is a potential summer target for Newcastle, talkSPORT understands.

Delap met with Magpies boss Eddie Howe before joining Chelsea from Ipswich last summer and was impressed with the Newcastle boss.

4 Chelsea's Liam Delap has emerged as a summer target for Newcastle Credit: Getty

Chelsea are likely to be open to offers for Delap, who has scored only one Premier League goal this season.

The £40m-rated striker has struggled for form since his arrival at Stamford Bridge while injuries have also hampered his season.

Chelsea boss Liam Rosenior has relied on Joao Pedro since taking over in January with Delap playing a bit-part role.

Newcastle, meanwhile, are looking to revamp their attack despite splashing £120million on Nick Woltemade and Yoane Wissa last summer.

Newcastle boss Eddie Howe has made bolstering his attack a top priority during the summer transfer window.

The departure of Alexander Isak last summer left Howe in limbo and short of attacking options with time running out in the window.

The towering German has netted ten times since arriving on Tyneside but only once since the start of 2026.

Meanwhile, Wissa has found the net three times in 21 appearances for the Magpies this term.

The acquisitions of Woltemade and Wissa are yet to bear fruit with both players banished to the bench for both legs of Newcastle's Champions League clashes with Barcelona.

Both players were unused substitutes during Wednesday's 7-2 defeat at the Nou Camp.

4 The goals have come to a screeching halt for £69m signing Nick Woltemade Credit: Getty

4 Yoane Wissa has struggled to recapture his prolific form of last season Credit: Getty

Woltemade is believed to be frustrated at his lack of game time, particularly in the club's key fixtures.

Howe had opted to deploy Anthony Gordon as a false nine, while Will Osula has also been preferred of late following his last-gasp heroics against Manchester United.

Despite only joining Newcastle in August, Woltemade's future has been consistently under the microscope.

Bayern Munich are understood to be monitoring his situation following their interest last summer.

Regardless of which players are shipped out this summer, Howe is working to identify a new striker to lead the line next season.

Former England Under-21 star Delap, impressed during his first Premier League campaign with Ipswich, scoring 12 goals in 37 appearances for the Tractor Boys.

4 Liam Delap shot to fame with Ipswich earning himself a move to Stamford Bridge last summer Credit: Getty

Newcastle were one of a host of teams interested in signing the Manchester City academy star before his move to Stamford Bridge and their interest has not cooled.

Inside The Toon on talkSPORT talkSPORT does Newcastle like nowhere else. Stay ahead of every tactical shift, manager sacking and transfer rumour by making talkSPORT's Inside The Toon show your weekly go to for all things Magpies related. Subscribe now to ensure you never miss a beat of the action one episode at a time, sponsored by BetMGM. Download the app or visit BetMGM.co.uk. 18+gambleaware.org

Inside The Toon verdict

Newcastle's interest in Delap is damning for the future of Woltemade and Wissa at the club.

Both of them have sat on the bench in recent games with Gordon and Will Osula often preferred.

Speaking on Inside The Toon, Head of Sport at The Sun, Shaun Custis, said: “We spent £120m on two blokes who just sat on the bench. We can’t go on about tiredness if we’ve got £120m worth of players just sitting there, not trusted, to take on this role to get us through.”

Newcastle fan Adam Cleary added: “We have seen this season that Woltemade has got his plus points, but he’s not a great active presser from the front of the pitch. That’s not what his game is.

“We don’t believe Wissa is, despite being a lot better at it at Brentford, but that might be down to the injury. Maybe he’s just not trusted.

“They have wasted quite a bit of money in the summer for what they need in this part of the season.”

News.title: Britain’s housing and welfare systems risk being ‘overwhelmed’ without tougher migrant rules
News.content:
BRITAIN’S housing and welfare systems risk being “overwhelmed” without tougher migrant settlement rules, a Home Office source claims.

It comes as Labour is engulfed in a civil war over plans to double the time when migrants already in the UK can apply for indefinite leave to remain.



Former Deputy PM Angela Rayner branded the policy “un-British” and has the backing of around 100 MPs from the Left of the party.



But a Home Office source said: “We’ve got up to 1.6million people on track to settle by 2030, and welfare and housing are already under serious strain, as every constituency MP knows from their inbox.

“If we don’t act now, that pressure will risk getting out of control.

“We’re making sure the system can cope, while keeping a fair route to settlement for those who contribute.”

The row centres on the record “Boriswave” of migrant workers and their families from 2021 to 2024, so named as it began when Boris Johnson was Prime Minister.

Many are nearing the point where they can apply to stay indefinitely, unlocking access to benefits and social housing.

Under Labour’s plans, the time here before settlement would double from five to ten years.

The main flashpoint is whether this will apply to migrants here now.

Ministers are reviewing responses to a major consultation on whether and how changes should apply to those here already.

News.title: ‘Demoralising’ holiday tax will hit family breaks in the UK, warns Labour MP
News.content:
A LABOUR MP has broken ranks to oppose a “demoralising” holiday tax, warning it will hit family breaks in the UK.

Emma Lewell is opposing plans to give mayors powers to levy a “per person, per night” charge on hotels and other tourist accommodation.



She has written to Chancellor Rachel Reeves raising “serious” concerns about the proposals.



The South Shields MP said: “A ‘per person, per night’ charge will quickly add up for a family.

“When households are already under pressure with the cost of living rises, this is demoralising and unaffordable.

“Families need a break. Taxing their break is a step too far.”

Hospitality bosses say the tax could add £100 to a two-week break and drive tourists abroad.

A Government spokesperson said: “We expect any new charges to be modest, and for mayors to consider the right level for their area.”

Major firms including Butlin’s, Hilton and Travelodge have responded to the proposals.

They say the plans would drain cash from local businesses and make the UK less competitive.

News.title: From Flat White or F**k Off Ltd to Phat B*****d Burger, all the cheeky UK business names BANNED by watchdog
News.content:
YOU often have to take a chance in business – but coming up with names such as Bitchface Training Company, Bloody Noora Ltd and Willys Bottles is pushing it.

Those efforts to stand out from the crowd have got the red card — along with Hypno Bimbo Sluts Ltd, Phat Bastrd Burger and Take Your Sh!t To The Next Level Ltd.





They were among 464 risque business names vetoed in 2025 after they were ruled too rude to appear on the official Companies House register.

And other ambitious suggestions ruled out by officials included Drone Strike Financing Company, Con Artists Limited and Meth Express.

An entrepreneur who tried to take on Starbucks and Costa with a cafe called Flat White Or Fck Off Limited was also stopped in their tracks.

Drug-related names blocked included Cannabis Investment Corporation, Crack Cookies Ltd and Ketamine Education Services.

Officials also had a downer on Ganja Ltd, the Meow Meow Cafe and UK LSD Ltd.

More would-be company names that raised red flags were Mafia Ltd, Swastika Properties and Isis Guerrero Ltd. Cheeky or crude titles such as Ass Tasty Buns Ltd, Cocks Catering Ltd, Quick N Dirty Ltd, Anuservices Ltd and Taking The Piss Ltd also failed to make the grade.

More oddball business names deemed unsuitable for the register were Stan And The Floozy Ltd, The Fat Tart Ltd, Smellyballs Ltd and The Old Bastard Gin Company.



Companies House has strict rules designed to stop names that could cause concern or imply criminal activity.

Its test is whether a “reasonable person would take offence”.

Every year, hundreds of hopeful founders fall foul of the restrictions when their chosen name is ruled unacceptable.

In previous years, officials have said no to names such as Bell End Motor Company Ltd, Phat Phuc Noodle Bar, Sticky Dicks Pub Company Ltd and Milfclub Underwear Ltd.

A Companies House spokesman said: “We have a statutory responsibility to ensure that the names we register do not have the potential to offend.



“All applications are carefully considered, but we will not register one where the name as a whole is considered offensive.”

News.title: Key part of epic Battle of Hastings story ‘exposed as a MYTH’ in ‘exciting’ new discovery
News.content:
KING Harold’s 200-mile march to Hastings in 1066 is a myth, claims a historian.

New research suggests his army sailed there before Harold is said to have died from an arrow in the eye in battle with William the Conqueror.





Prof Tom Licence cast doubt on the theory that Harold had already dismissed his fleet at the South Coast, leaving him no choice but to hurry his troops from Yorkshire.

The Anglo-Saxon Chronicle, which recorded the events, stated that the ships “came home” — a phrase Victorian historians believe meant that he disbanded his navy.

Instead Mr Licence says the ships sailed north to back Harold’s fight against Norway’s Harald Hardrada, then returned to their London base with his men aboard — before continuing to Hastings in East Sussex.

He said: “I checked the evidence for him having sent the fleet home and found it was just a misunderstanding. I went looking in the sources for evidence of a forced march and there wasn’t any.



“Harold’s campaign was not a desperate dash across England — it was a sophisticated land-sea operation.”

Harold first defeated Norwegian invaders at Stamford Bridge, East Yorks.

The idea of weary men then marching 200 miles in ten days on medieval roads is implausible, said Prof Licence, of the University of East Anglia.

He said: “Only a mad general would have sent all his men on foot in this way if ships were available.”



The Bayeux Tapestry, depicting the battle, travels from France to the British Museum later this year.

English Heritage’s Roy Porter said: “Harold’s response may have been far more sophisticated than previously understood.”

News.title: OAP ‘crushed to death by 20 tonne digger’ on busy high street as kids ‘screamed & cried’ after man went ‘underneath’
News.content:
A PENSIONER was crushed to death by a 20-tonne digger on a busy London street while children looking on screamed and cried.

The 70-year-old man was crossing the road when he was hit by the Volvo digger and ended up completely underneath it.





Police have arrested a 61-year-old man on suspicion of causing death by dangerous driving and he remains in custody.

The horrific crash occurred just after 11.30am on Thursday on Kilburn High Street in North London.

Police erected a cordon and set up a forensic tent in front of the digger which was surrounded by police tape.

Harjeet Shekarpouri, 21, was working in Fone Vape across from the crash

He told Metro: “It was horrible, there was screaming and a woman shouting ‘move’ to the driver.

“The poor man was underneath. People were crying. I’ve never seen anything like that, I can’t get it out of my head.”

He added that he couldn’t see how the elderly victim could have survived the immense weight of the digger.

Christopher Todorov told the paper he heard shouting, screaming and children crying after the smash.



Kilburn High Street was closed while police carried out an investigation.

The traffic caused chaos for commuters as drivers were diverted around the crash site.

The Metropolitan Police have urged anyone with information or footage of the crash to get in touch with the force.

A spokesperson said: ‘Police were called to reports of a road traffic collision between a heavy plant vehicle and a pedestrian on Kilburn High Road.

“Officers attended the scene alongside the London Ambulance Service and London’s Air Ambulance.



“Sadly, the pedestrian, a 70-year-old man, died at the scene.

“The driver, a 61-year-old man, stopped at the scene. He was arrested on suspicion of causing death by dangerous driving and taken into police custody, where he remains.”

A London Ambulance Service spokesperson told Construction News: “The first paramedic arrived at the scene in around five minutes. Despite the efforts of our team, a person sadly died at the scene.”

They added that two ambulances, three paramedics in rapid response cars and an incident response officer all scrambled to the scene.

News.title: The massive energy shock heading our way should shatter Labour’s fantasy economics once and for all
News.content:
Paying the price

THE war on Iran is piling up problems for Rachel Reeves.

The Chancellor was never going to be able to tax and spend her way out of her economic bind.



Now the missiles raining down on energy plants across the Gulf threaten to shoot her plans to pieces.



Government borrowing figures released yesterday were truly horrendous — another £14.3billion added to Britain’s mountain of debt.

Labour likes to remind voters of how Liz Truss’s economic meltdown was triggered by her losing the faith of the bond markets.

Yesterday, gilts surged to a level not seen since the 2008 crash.

Worryingly, Britain’s debt-to-GDP ratio under Truss was only HALF what it is today.

The only way out of Britain’s economic decline is to curb spending and grow the economy. So far, Labour has instead chosen to hike taxes to record levels.

The Government takes in £331billion in income tax but then splurges £333billion on welfare alone.

That’s why it’s imperative that Sir Keir Starmer and his Chancellor face down left-wing MPs who demand MORE handouts and MORE spending.

The massive energy shock heading our way should shatter their fantasy economics once and for all.



In same boat

UPON his appointment as Border Security Commander, ex-cop Martin Hewitt told The Sun he would be judged on his ability to stop small boat migrant crossings.

Given that over 55,000 entered illegally on his watch, it is no surprise he’s gone.

Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood apparently wants more of a hands-on approach. In turn, Hewitt is said to have quit after becoming “frustrated”.

Whatever the truth behind his exit, it has once again raised the impossibility of stopping the small boats without a meaningful deterrent.

Asking French police for help doesn’t work. The one-in, one-out scheme will do nothing to cut numbers.



Until the Government finds a proper brake — as the Rwanda scheme might have been — nothing is likely to change.

And Hewitt’s replacement is likely to suffer the exact same fate.

Better or worse

WHEN the Tory Government proposed new affordability checks on gambling, it was agreed they should only be introduced if “frictionless” for punters.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

The minister at the time, Nigel Huddleston, now says a pilot of the checks by the Gambling Commission is “not working” — and would drive people to the black market, depriving the Treasury of £200million in tax revenue.

Ideally, the Commission will think again. Failing that, the Chancellor must force the quango to see sense.

News.title: How Iran war has blown apart the vast delusions of the British state – and sent our reputation down the pan
News.content:
TODAY marks three weeks since America and Israel’s shock and awe blitz on Iran began.

The bloodthirsty Ayatollah Khamenei is in the ground.



And there’s no real sign of life from the colourful son who replaced him as Supreme Leader, either.



Mojtaba Khamenei may have lost a leg, his head or both.

He stepped into the garden just as the Israeli missiles were falling on his complex, and he has not been seen since.

Possibly in a coma, possibly even unaware he is even in charge.



All this possibly by design, as the Mad Mullahs scrabble to present a brave and united front after scores of Iran’s senior leadership were smoked in a matter of hours.

More than 5,000 bombs worth over $16billion fell on Iran in the first days of the assault that the Foreign Policy Research Institute says was “the most intensive opening air campaign in modern history”.

There is no doubt that the opening salvos of this war were a success. However, the latest developments remain highly contested.

Those hoping a regime that has spent nearly half a century ratcheting its evil grip on its people would simply melt away have clearly been caught short.



Yet some will never see this war as anything more than an unmitigated disaster, such is their loathing of Donald Trump or the Jewish state of Israel.

But Iran’s resistance HAS upset the global applecart amid soaring oil prices, borrowing costs and the cruel spectre of inflation once again looming over our wallets.

On Saves the West this week, former Naval Intelligence Officer Malcolm Nance warned that whacking the Ayatollah has made the world a more dangerous place.

He compared it to replacing an old banger with the “Lamborghini of terrorism” – younger, angrier and ready to take Iran’s self-declared War on the US into a third generation.

He insists that the regime in Tehran is playing a game of “strategic confusion” with its missing leader, who — despite being absent from the airwaves — has brought the Strait of Hormuz to a close and the global oil market to its knees.



Others suggest that is a glossy reimagining of a broken regime that is seeing the Arab world unite against it.

Only time will tell.

In Washington, despite President Trump’s continued upbeat assessment that America is winning, the closure of the Strait is clearly hurting.

Last night, an increasingly snappy President lashed out again at Nato allies for refusing his plea to help police the crucial trade route for most of the world’s oil exports.

“Without the U.S.A., NATO IS A PAPER TIGER”, Trump screeched on his Truth Social site. “They complain about the high oil prices they are forced to pay, but don’t want to help open the Strait of Hormuz. So easy for them to do, with so little risk. COWARDS, and we will REMEMBER!”



Yet contrary to the narrative, Trump’s most loyal voters back the President, despite his promise to end “forever wars” in the Middle East.

Loud podcasters and influencers, however, have cut up rough and are making plenty of noise — but successive polls show they are out of kilter with the actual base.

JL Partners found just eight per cent of voters who identify as Maga side with figures like Tucker Carlson and Megyn Kelly, who are leading the vocal charge against the war.



CNN found similar rock solid support amongst Trump’s core — but his backing has plummeted with swing voters who gave him back power in 2024.

Yet the wider American public IS sceptical of the war, with those fractures boiling over in the administration — most notably with this week’s resignation of Trump’s National Counterterrorism Center director.

Joe Kent risked allegations of anti-Semitism by blaming a powerful Israeli lobby for forcing Trump into the war, saying the threat from Iran was “cooked up”.

And he raged that his “ability to have my voice heard, to present data that runs contrary to the trajectory and the agenda that the administration is on . . . was squashed before it even really reaches the White House.”

The administration say that wacky Kent had already been sidelined, hence why his voice was not being heard.



But even if that is the case, icing out your top counter terror adviser is hardly a sign that all is well on the inside.

Closer to home, Trump’s war has brought several vast delusions of the British state to a juddering halt, too.

Our suicidal energy policy leaves us over-reliant on costly oil and gas imports, virtue signalling as we shiver into bankruptcy and irrelevance.

Even if we were not led by a high priest of the mythical international order, our hollowed out defences would have been incapable of offering any meaningful support to the war.

As we pay to give away Chagos, we couldn’t even defend our base in Cyprus.



Heaven knows what the Argies must be thinking about the Falklands these days as they watch the once great Royal Navy struggle to launch onto the waves it once ruled.

Sir Keir Starmer’s cherished status as a bridge between Trump’s America and Europe is in tatters, while our anaemic economy is simply not prepared for another global shock.

Whether the PM likes it or not, this is becoming our war.

John Bolton, the former US National Security Adviser to Trump, warns retaliation from Tehran risks blood on the streets of Europe, telling me yesterday: “We should all be on a heightened state of alert.

“Given the pummelling the Iranians are taking, their resort to asymmetric warfare, particularly terrorist acts, is almost guaranteed.



“This idea that this is not Europe’s war is just wrong. This affects all of us, as we can see from the impact on the global economy.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

So, the terror threat is up, prices are up, borrowing costs are up, energy bills are up . . . and Britain’s reputation is down the pan.

It’s not yet clear how the war in Iran ends, just as it’s unclear how we handle another three weeks of it.

News.title: Chilling moment thief steals car with two-month-old baby still inside from in front of its terrified mother
News.content:
THIS is the chilling moment a man steals a car with a two-month-old baby boy still inside, in front of his terrified mother.

Horrifying footage shows the baby’s mum step out of her bright red motor to pick up her other child from an after-school activity centre in São Paulo, Brazil.





She walks around the car and stands in front of it.

But mere seconds later, a man creeps into view and gets into the motor.

The mum’s back is turned.

Completely unaware that a stranger has got into her car with her baby, she chats on the pavement, waiting for her older child.

As she turns towards the street with her seven-year-old daughter, the red car reverses.

The mum looks confused and seems to try and speak to the suspected thief.

She looks on, astonished, as he speeds away with her car and her baby.



CCTV shows the woman seeming to chase after the vehicle and plead with onlookers for help – some join the pursuit while others simply watch in horror.

A staff member ushers terrified children off the street and into the gates of the after-school activity centre.

The suspect tried to get away but didn’t make it far because of traffic, cops said.

He then abandoned the baby in the car and attempted to flee on foot.

But he slipped and fell – the ground was wet from heavy rain that morning.



Cops said he tried to get away one final time, but his escape was thwarted by members of the public who held him down, before police arrested the 26-year-old suspect at the scene.

The baby was recovered around a mile and a half away from the activity centre and reunited with his distraught mother.

He was taken to hospital for a check-up, but escaped the terrifying ordeal unscathed.

The baby’s mum, Maria Samara, said she stepped out of the car to help her daughter with her school bag.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Visibly emotional, she told G1: “I do this every day. But today, unfortunately, there was someone who shouldn’t have been there at that moment.



“Before I even turned my back to the car, he got in, and took my son with him.”

News.title: British Airways statement as passengers 'spend 13 hours on plane with dead body'
News.content:
British Airways statement as passengers 'spend 13 hours on plane with dead body'

Reports claim a woman died an hour into a British Airways flight from Hong Kong to London Heathrow - and now the airline has issued a statement

View 2 Images The woman died on a British Airways flight (stock) (Image: Mrkit99 via Getty Images)

British Airways has issued a statement after a dead passenger was reportedly left in a galley for 13 hours on a flight.

‌



The woman, in her 60s, died an hour into a journey from Hong Kong to London Heathrow. The pilots of the Airbus A350-1000 reportedly continued the flight, and there have now been claims from passengers about a "foul smell" from the area.

‌



An unnamed source told The Sun the family with the woman were distraught, and so were the crew. They added that many passengers wanted to go back to Hong Kong, but said the death of a passenger is not considered an emergency.

‌



British Airways spokesman told the Express : "A customer sadly passed away on board and our thoughts are with their family and friends at this difficult time. We are supporting our crew and all procedures were correctly followed."

View 2 Images BA issued a statement (stock) (Image: Ceri Breeze via Getty Images)

The source said the flight deck wanted the body to be stored in a toilet, but that proposal was rejected by crew members. Instead the body was wrapped up and moved to a galley at the back of the jet, according to the same source.

‌



They said by the end of the journey there was a "foul smell" in the vicinity of the galley, which had a heated floor. When the plane landed in the UK, police went on board to investigate, keeping passengers on board and in their seats for 45 minutes.

The source claimed: “A discussion was had about what to do with the body — with the flightdeck’s request to lock it in a toilet rejected by crew. So they had to isolate the body, wrap it in materials, and move it to a galley at the rear of the plane.

“The galley had a heated floor, which some crew had overlooked, and towards the end of the flight there were claims that a foul smell was present in that region.”

Article continues below

Deaths on flights are rare, according to a study published by the New England Journal of Medicine in 2013, cited by the BBC. A majority of airlines follow International Air Transport Association guidance on what to do if a passenger dies on a flight.

The guidance states when a passenger is presumed to have died, their eyes should be closed and their body placed in a body bag, if one is available.

If there is no body bag, then the body should be covered with a blanket up to the neck. They should be moved to a seat or area away from other passengers if possible too.

News.title: Kim Jong-un beams as his teen 'heir' daughter drives him in an anti-drone tank
News.content:
Kim Jong-un beams as his teen 'heir' daughter drives him in an anti-drone tank

North Korean leader Kim Jong-un took his daughter to another official appearance as the pair, in matching outfits, observed military drills before the teenager rode a war tank

View 4 Images Kim Jon-un sat on a tank being driven by his teenage daughter (Image: KCNA VIA KNS/AFP via Getty Images)

North Korean dictator Kim Jong-un grinned while he sat on a tank as his teenager daughter drove him on the war machine.

‌



Kim Ju Ae, believed to be around 13, has been pictured more with her father at official events as the unpredictable despot appears to have chosen her as his heir.

‌



The duo were snapped in North Korea's latest display of military strength - a large-scale military drill. The teenage girl's head was seen peering out from the front seat of the massive tank while her dad calmly sat on the war machine. Three other North Korean military officials were also sat on the machine as the teen took them for a spin.

‌



View 4 Images The dictator smiling while his daughter showed off the new tanks (Image: KCNA VIA KNS/AFP via Getty Images)

Footage from the state news agency Korean Central News Agency showed the bizarre moment the teenager jumped into the tank's hatchet while her dad leaned against the turret and smiled on the journey.

The dad and daughter duo, who were wearing matching leather jackets and outfits, had been supervising a drill of a new type of tank. This fresh model has more advanced offence and defence capabilities against drones and anti-tanks systems.

‌



The pair also oversaw rocket launches and drones in action among other military demonstrations in Pyongyang, on Thursday. The North Korean state media claimed these weapons and devices were key in the country's military modernisation.

View 4 Images Jong-un has brought his teenager along to another appearance (Image: AP)

The North Korean training, meanwhile, came as the U.S. and South Korea were engaged in their annual military exercises that North Korea views as an invasion rehearsal.

‌



The U.S. and South Korea wrapped up their 11-day computer-simulated command post exercise on Thursday, but they are still continuing field training.

This is yet another important event that Jong-un has brought his young daughter to. In the footage and images shared online by North Korea, the state media called Ju-ae the dictator's "respected" kid, which is a term reserved for North Korea's most venerated figures, suggesting how important she has become to Kim's strict and barbaric regime.

The teen's existence was discovered when former NBA star, and odd friend of Jong-un, Dennis Rodman publicly revealed the despot had more than one child. Rodman visited Pyongyang in 2013 and afterwards said he enjoyed meeting the daughter and Kim's wife.

Article continues below

View 4 Images The dad and daughter observing a drill (Image: KCNA VIA KNS/AFP via Getty Images)

At the time, the basketball star told The Guardian: "I held their baby Ju Ae and spoke with (Kim's wife) as well." The daughter made her first public appearance in November 2022, at the launch of an intercontinental ballistic missile.

Just a few months later, the young girl was being featured on postage stamps in North Korea and even attending high level banquets. Since then, the teen accompanied her dad on a rare appearance outside of North Korea to China for a military parade. They were also pictured firing pistols together at a munitions factory's shooting range last week.

News.title: UK airline plunged into administration after 28 years – all flights cancelled
News.content:
UK airline plunged into administration after 28 years – all flights cancelled

Eastern Airways first suspended operations last year before entering administration - the airline was in business from 1997 and was based at Humberside Airport in North Lincolnshire

View 2 Images Eastern Airways suspended operations in October (stock) (Image: Getty Images/iStockphoto)

A UK airline has plunged into administration having been in business since 1997.

‌



Eastern Airways first suspended operations in October before entering administration in November. It had been based at Humberside Airport in North Lincolnshire and operated regional services from airports across the United Kingdom.

‌



All of its flights were cancelled as a result. It had services right across the UK, Europe and Ireland. Administrators said the airline was left with a cost base that was "too high to be sustainable" after a contract to operate services for Dutch airline KLM was ended.

‌



Speaking to the BBC at the time, Jamie Miller from RSM UK Restructuring Advisory, who was appointed joint administrator, said sufficient staff had been retained to maintain the fleet while they sought to rescue some or all of Eastern's operations.

View 2 Images All of its flights were cancelled (file) (Image: Aero Pixels from England, CC BY 2.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0>, via Wikimedia Commons)

He added: "We would welcome any interest from potential alternative operators, or those who may have an interest in the underlying assets."

‌



RSM said Eastern Airways had been operating four aircraft for KLM Cityhopper in Europe, but when this contract was terminated, it had left Eastern with "high fixed overheads and a staff base that has ultimately proved too high to be sustainable". The majority of its 330 staff were reportedly made redundant.

Quoted by Travel Weekly, Mr Miller also said: “It is extremely sad that such a long established and historically successful independent airline, one of the few remaining in the UK, has had to enter administration.

"The unexpected and sudden termination of Eastern’s KLM contract, along with other economic factors, unfortunately left the directors with no choice but to appoint administrators.

‌



"At its peak, Eastern was an award-winning airline providing 200 flights per day. They also provided valuable services on public service obligation (PSO) routes and supported energy critical services to the oil and gas sector."

This comes after Royal Air Philippines was forced to cancel all of its commercial flights when it entered administration. Between 3,000 and 4,000 passengers holding bookings from January through March were reportedly left stranded.

Royal Air Philippines CEO Eduardo Novillas had already signalled weak demand weeks ago, warning the carrier would halt commercial flights by January 4 in a letter to a travel agency ahead of Christmas, Philstar reported.

Article continues below

He pointed to “significantly low” interest from key markets. Asian Development Bank economist Jules Hugot noted arrivals from China to the Philippines remained well below pre Covid pandemic figures entering early 2025.

News.title: Donald Trump 'plotting invasion of Iran's Kharg Island' as marines hours away
News.content:
Donald Trump is mulling plans to seize the small Gulf island that is central to Iran’s oil export system, according to US sources.

The Trump administration is said to be weighing options to occupy or blockade Kharg Island in a bid to pressure Tehran into reopening the Strait of Hormuz, four sources with knowledge of the matter told US outlet Axios.

Kharg Island, located in the Persian Gulf, lies around 16 miles off the Iranian coast and roughly 300 miles north-west of the Strait of Hormuz. The island processes about 90 per cent of Iran's crude oil exports and has long been vital in connecting the country's oil reserves with the global market.

"He [Trump] wants Hormuz open. If he has to take Kharg Island to make it happen, that's going to happen," a senior administration official told Axios. "If he decides to have a coastal invasion, that's going to happen. But that decision hasn't been made.

Image: EUROPEAN SPACE AGENCY/AFP via Ge) EUROPEAN SPACE AGENCY/AFP via Ge)

Officials have warned that occupying or blockading the island would put US troops at serious risk. Such an operation would therefore only be launched once US forces have degraded Iran’s military capacity around the strait, Axios reports.

"We need about a month to weaken the Iranians more with strikes, take the island and then get them by the balls and use it for negotiations," a source told the outlet.

Image: 2026 Planet Labs PBC/AFP via Get) 2026 Planet Labs PBC/AFP via Get)

But others warned that such a high-risk move could backfire. Rear Admiral (Ret.) Mark Montgomery told Axios: "If we seize Kharg Island, they're going to turn off the spigot on the other end. It's not like we control their oil production."

Kharg Island has massive storage tanks and pipelines capable of handling millions of barrels of crude oil daily. Large oil tankers regularly dock there to transport oil extracted from fields in southwestern Iran. The island was targeted during the eight-year Iran-Iraq War, due to its strategic economic importance.

It comes as three more warships and around 2,500 more marines are making their way to the Middle East, a US official confirmed to The Associated Press. Three sources told Axios that an occupation of the island by ground troops is being considered, as well as the options to impose a naval blockade and prevent tankers from reaching the island.

One source told Axios that the Marines arriving in days could carry out other operations, including evacuating embassy staff in the region if needed. The US military says it already has about 50,000 troops stationed in the region. The Pentagon’s request for another $200 billion to fund the war would need congressional approval as the US national debt hits a record $39 trillion.

News.title: Student, 20, died 'oozing blood from her eyes' after contracting meningitis B infection
News.content:
Student, 20, died 'oozing blood from her eyes' after contracting meningitis B infection

Sophie Ward was rushed to A&E after showing symptoms of meningitis at university, but she was turned away - and the 20-year-old tragically died hours later, it's been revealed

View 4 Images Sophie Ward died 'bleeding from her eyes' after contracting Meningitis B (Image: Paul Ward/My London)

The parents of a student, 20, who died after contracting meningitis B at university have demanded more action to protect young people from the deadly infection.

‌



Sophie Ward, 20, died with "blood oozing from her eyes" hours after she was misdiagnosed with the flu at an A&E department then discharged. The 20-year-old and her mum had rushed to A&E at Barnet Hospital on the advice of 111 and told medics Sophie's symptoms fitted those of meningitis.

‌



At 2am the next morning she was "violently sick" as her temperature soared to 39.4C, so she was rushed back to hospital but it was too late to save her - Sophie died around 12 hours later, on July 31, 2023. Sophie's dad said the "government needs to step up" amid an outbreak in Kent which killed two students last week.

‌



View 4 Images The 20-year-old had been misdiagnosed by medics (Image: Paul Ward/My London)

Juliette Kenny, an 18-year-old sixth form student, tragically died on Saturday March 14 after contracting the deadly infection. A 21-year-old student from the University of Kent also died amid the outbreak linked to the Club Chemistry nightclub, in Canterbury.

Sophie, an artist from London, tragically died less than 24 hours after she started to show symptoms. When Sophie's condition deteriorated after coming home her mother, Alice, frantically dialled 999 and an ambulance arrived within 15 minutes, racing Sophie to resuscitation while "blood seemed to ooze from her eyes", an inquest into her death heard.

‌



View 4 Images Juliette Kenny, 18, sadly died on Saturday after contracting meningitis

Nearly three years after Sophie's death, her father Paul Ward said he did not realise his daughter wasn't fully protected from meningitis because she had the jabs for A, C, W and Y strains as a teen. The menB jab was introduced on the NHS for babies in 2015, meaning the majority of young people born before then are not protected against it unless they have had the jab privately.

Mr Ward, speaking to the Daily Mail, said: "Young people and their parents don't realise they aren't protected against meningitis B. The government has allowed that state of affairs to persist.

‌



"They so far haven't done anything to inform people that they aren't protected against meningitis. The government needs to step up - even if they don't make meningitis B what's called a scheduled vaccine, they could do something in between."

View 4 Images Students queuing to receive vaccines and antibiotics at the University of Kent (Image: PA)

Just days before the Kent outbreak started, Mr Ward had appeared on ITV News to call for more young people to get the menB jab. He said: "Sophie should be alive, and if she had had the vaccine for menB she would be alive. This devastation - it doesn't have to happen."

Article continues below

An inquest, last year, into Sophie's death heard how the Wimbledon College of Art student was seen two hours later which, retrospectively, should have been 15 minutes when she was first admitted to A&E. Mum Alice told proceedings: "Sophie was a young women, almost 21-years-old…she was dearly loved and gave and received so much joy to her family who knew her so well.

"She was admired by all for her tenderness, her sincerity, her kindness, and her beauty. As a young woman at age of 16 she became adventurous and outgoing, with a wide circle of friends whose company she cherished." She added: "Her death was preventable and tragic, pain and sorrow that cannot be softened. She was loved and no good comes from her death, but change must come about in hospital diagnoses and protocols."

News.title: Donald Trump says he is 'considering winding down Iran war' in message to allies
News.content:
Donald Trump says he is 'considering winding down Iran war' in message to allies

Donald Trump says he is considering "winding down" military efforts in the Middle East.

Trump's statement seemed at odds with his administration's move to send more troops and warships to the region and request another $200billion from Congress to fund the war against Iran.

Trump made the comment in a post on social media Friday evening after another climb in oil prices sent the US stock market sharply lower.

The president also made confusing statements over whether the US would police the vital Strait of Hormuz shipping lane. Trump had said this week that the US didn't need help, while also complaining that other countries did not help.

Trump wrote: "We are getting very close to meeting our objectives as we consider winding down our great Military efforts in the Middle East with respect to the Terrorist Regime of Iran: (1) Completely degrading Iranian Missile Capability, Launchers, and everything else pertaining to them.

"(2) Destroying Iran’s Defense Industrial Base. (3) Eliminating their Navy and Air Force, including Anti Aircraft Weaponry. (4) Never allowing Iran to get even close to Nuclear Capability, and always being in a position where the U.S.A. can quickly and powerfully react to such a situation, should it take place. (5) Protecting, at the highest level, our Middle Eastern Allies, including Israel, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the United Arab Emirates, Bahrain, Kuwait, and others.

"The Hormuz Strait will have to be guarded and policed, as necessary, by other Nations who use it — The United States does not! If asked, we will help these Countries in their Hormuz efforts, but it shouldn’t be necessary once Iran’s threat is eradicated. Importantly, it will be an easy Military Operation for them. Thank you for your attention to this matter! President DONALD J. TRUMP."

Image: Getty) Getty)

Earlier, Trump said the UK “should have acted a lot faster” in allowing America to use British bases to strike Iranian missile sites targeting the Strait of Hormuz.

Ministers agreed on Friday afternoon that UK bases could be used for US “defensive operations to degrade the missile sites and capabilities being used to attack ships”.

Up until this point, the Government had allowed the US to use British bases – including the one on Diego Garcia – only to hit missile sites targeting British interests in the region.

Speaking to reporters outside the White House, Trump said: “It’s been a very late response from the UK. I’m surprised because the relationship is so good, but this has never happened before. They were really, pretty much our first ally, all over the world.”

Referring to Diego Garcia, he said: “They didn’t want us to use the island, the so called island, which for some reason they gave up rights to it. I was a little surprised in the UK, to be honest. They should have acted a lot faster.”

News.title: Royal vows to 'take responsibility' for Epstein friendship in tense TV grilling
News.content:
Royal vows to 'take responsibility' for Epstein friendship in tense TV grilling

Crown Princess Mette-Marit has said she feels it's 'important to take responsibility' after not looking into paedophile Jeffrey Epstein's background before befriending him

View 6 Images Crown Princess Mette-Marit became emotional as she said she regretted her friendship with Jeffrey Epstein (Image: BBC)

A royal says it's "important" to take responsibility after befriending Jeffrey Epstein. Crown Princess Mette-Marit of Norway has given a bombshell interview surrounding her bond with the convicted paedophile.

The future Queen of Norway said she wished she had never met Epstein, not long after her links to the disgraced financier were revealed in the latest batch of Epstein files, released by the US Department of Justice. The new files suggested a long-running friendship between the princess and Epstein.

In a new interview, filmed at the royal residence in Skaugum, Mette-Marit became visibly emotional as she sat alongside her husband, Crown Prince Haakon. She told public broadcaster NRK that she regrets her "very friendly relationship" with Epstein.

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

View 6 Images Crown Princess Mette-Marit of Norway says she regrets her friendship with Jeffrey Epstein (Image: BBC)

Epstein took his own life aged 66 at the Metropolitan Correctional Centre in New York in August 2019. She went on to add: "Of course, I wish I had never met him. I feel so manipulated, and when you are manipulated, you don’t realise it from the start."

Mette-Marit, 52, continued: "It is incredibly important for me to take responsibility for not checking his background more carefully. And to take responsibility for being so manipulated and deceived as I was." In an email to Epstein, she told the convicted sex offender, "you make me smile," while he stated he was on a "wife hunt".

She went on to tell Epstein that Paris was "good for adultery," and suggested that Scandinavian women were "better wife material." Mette-Marit stated that she "didn't know he was a sex offender or predator" throughout the duration of her friendship with Epstein.

View 6 Images Mette-Marit's husband, Crown Prince Haakon is the future King of Norway, meaning she will be one day be Queen (Image: BBC)

They emailed one another between 2011 and 2014. She also stayed at his Florida home while he was away. In 2008, Epstein was convicted of soliciting a minor for prostitution, and made a plea deal which made him sign a register as a sex offender.

When it was pointed out in the 20-minute interview that Epstein's conviction was listed on his Wikipedia page, Mette-Marit said: "I can't remember this; it was 15 years ago. I still didn't know anything about all the abuse. But I had understood enough that I thought he was a bad guy who people shouldn't have contact with. And I had seen up close how he blackmailed others. So I regret that I didn't tell more people, because I should have."

But in 2011, she sent an email to Epstein, reading: "Googled u after last email. Agree didn't look too good." She was asked why she stayed at his home in 2013, and said it was down to the pair having mutual friends. "Epstein was a close friend of a good friend of mine," she stated.

View 6 Images The princess sat alongside her husband as she recalled an incident which left her feeling 'unsafe' (Image: BBC)

But there was one "situation" which made her feel uneasy during her final day in Florida – although she refused to give an insight into it, only stating she phoned her husband, the future King of Norway.

Crown Prince Haakon said that the details of the incident made Mette-Marit feel "unsafe". While Mette-Marit remained in contact with Epstein after the incident in question, she eventually terminated their friendship. "I am overly trusting," she said, adding: "I tend to think the best of people.

"But I also chose to end all direct contact with him. And it was because of such episodes as that." Mette-Marit faced intense public scrutiny, including from Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre, after her link to Epstein became public. Mette-Marit also addressed whether she will continue in her royal role, given her link to Epstein and her ongoing health issues. This, however, would be determined by her health.

6 View 6 Images Epstein took his own life in 2019 (Image: New York State Sex Offender Regi) 6 View 6 Images Princess Mette-Marit faced intense scrutiny from the government and the Norwegian public (Image: Corbis via Getty Images)

In a previous statement, the Princess said, "Jeffrey Epstein is personally responsible for his actions. I must take responsibility for not checking Epstein's background more closely and for not understanding quickly enough what kind of person he was. I deeply regret that, and this is a responsibility I must take.

"I showed poor judgment and regret having any contact with Epstein at all. It is simply embarrassing. I want to express my deep sympathy and solidarity with the victims of the abuses committed by Jeffrey Epstein."

The interview was filmed on Thursday – the same day her son, Marius Borh Høiby's week-long rape trial concluded. Høiby pleaded not guilty to four charges of rape. He pleaded guilty to several driving offences, an aggravated drug offence and breaking a restraining order and "partly" to threats and aggravated assault. The Norwegian royals were not present in court throughout any of the proceedings.

Article continues below

While his mum is the future Queen and his stepfather will eventually become King, Høiby carries no status and does not officially represent the royal family in any capacity. At the start of the trial, the royals said: "We are thinking a lot about all the parties involved in this matter. This is something that affects them, their families, those who love them. We care about them. We know that many of you are going through a difficult time right now."

If you're struggling and need to talk, the Samaritans operate a free helpline open 24/7 on 116 123. Alternatively, you can email jo@samaritans.org or visit their site to find your local branch

Like this story? For more of the latest showbiz news and gossip, follow Mirror Celebs on TikTok, Snapchat, Instagram, Twitter, Facebook, YouTube and Threads.

News.title: Caernarfon fire as stunned Wetherspoons pubgoers film town centre inferno
News.content:
21:47 Ethan Blackshaw

How the fire in Caernarfon unfolded

Reports of a fire in the centre of Caernarfon emerged at around 8pm this evening. Harri Jones shared a video from inside the Tafarn Y Porth Wetherspoons showing flames ripping through an abandoned unit opposite the pub.

It is understood it is a former cafe site that has been empty for many years.

Pubgoers watched in shock as the blaze took hold. There were also concerns about people living in flats above the unit and also nearby properties.

Firefighters and police responsed. Police cordoned off the areas and crews tackled the blaze.

They were able to quickly get the fire under control. It looks like nearby proerties were relatively unscatched, North Wales Live reports.

News.title: 'I feel so grateful to be alive,' says Kent University meningitis survivor
News.content:
Twenty-one-year-old Annabelle Mackay has spoken to the BBC about her experience of surviving a meningitis infection.

The University of Kent law student believes she may have contracted it on 5 March at Club Chemistry, or possibly on an evening before this when she was out at a different venue.

So far, there have been 29 cases of meningitis linked to the outbreak, with a vaccination programme being rolled out for students.

"I feel so grateful to be alive and to be here. I'm still dealing with the aftereffects now, but I think I just need to focus on my recovery and getting better and back to myself," Mackay told the BBC.

Read more here about why this outbreak has spread so quickly, and read more here about the symptoms of the condition.
